title: Audit whether activeTab permission is necessary given existing host_permissions
description:
v2.3.0 added activeTab to manifest.json:24, intended to make popup auto-detection of the current tab URL more reliable (popup.js:120-130, tryPrefillFromActiveTab).

But host_permissions already include all matching hosts:
- manifest.json:27 https://x.com/*
- manifest.json:28 https://twitter.com/*
- manifest.json:30 https://mobile.twitter.com/*

chrome.tabs.query({active: true, currentWindow: true}) returns the URL field for tabs in granted hosts even without activeTab. So activeTab may be redundant.

Action:
- Test popup auto-prefill on x.com, twitter.com, and mobile.twitter.com WITHOUT activeTab (temporarily remove it from manifest, reload extension, observe).
- If prefill still works: remove activeTab for tighter least-privilege posture before Chrome Web Store review.
- If prefill breaks: keep it and document the reason in a comment in manifest.json or in REVIEW_AND_CHANGES_v2.3.0.md.

Files: manifest.json:24, popup.js:120-130.

title: Verify mobile.twitter.com inline-button injection works on real mobile DOM
description:
v2.3.0 added mobile.twitter.com to manifest.json:30 (host_permissions) and manifest.json:40 (content_scripts.matches). Popup/manual flow is verified by static analysis to work — the URL parser handles mobile.twitter.com correctly via the mobile.twitter.com$ allowance in buildTweetUrlCandidate (background.js:234, popup.js:425).

But content.js inline-button injection depends on X-desktop-flavored DOM selectors:
- content.js:10 SEL_TWEET_LINK
- content.js:11 SEL_TWEET_BLOCK ([data-testid='tweet'])
- content.js:12 SEL_GROUP ([role='group'])
- content.js:89-95 action-bar detection (>=4 children, >=4 interactive elements)

If mobile.twitter.com serves a different mobile DOM than the responsive desktop site, the script will inject but no inline button will appear. This was not validated end-to-end.

Action:
- Manually load https://mobile.twitter.com/<user>/status/<id> (or use Playwright via qa-kitten) with a video post, with the unpacked extension loaded in Chrome.
- Confirm the inline download button appears.
- Confirm Shift-click opens picker, Alt/Option-click opens Save As.
- If injection fails: either add mobile-specific selectors OR downgrade RELEASE_NOTES_v2.3.0.md and README.md to clarify mobile support is popup-only.

Note: x.com/twitter.com may already redirect mobile UA to the responsive site, so this may be a no-op in practice — investigate.

Files: content.js:10-12, content.js:89-95, manifest.json:30, manifest.json:40, background.js:234, popup.js:425.

title: Restrict bitrate string parsing to plain decimal digits
description:
background.js:373-374 parses MP4 variant bitrate via Number(variant.bitrate). This correctly rejects 'abc', negative, '0', and 'Infinity', but is permissive about non-decimal numeric forms:
- '1e6' → 1000000 (accepted)
- '0x10' → 16 (accepted)

The Twitter syndication API in practice returns plain decimal strings or numbers, so this is currently harmless. Variant URL is still locked to HTTPS Twimg via background.js:370. But if the spec is 'plain decimal bitrate string,' tighten the parse:

const rawBitrate = typeof variant.bitrate === 'number'
  ? variant.bitrate
  : (typeof variant.bitrate === 'string' && /^[1-9]\d*$/.test(variant.bitrate.trim())
      ? Number(variant.bitrate.trim())
      : NaN);
const bitrate = Number.isFinite(rawBitrate) && rawBitrate > 0 ? rawBitrate : 0;

Files: background.js:373-374.

title: Surface per-item downloadAll failures in popup status
description:
Background handleDownloadAll returns useful per-item errors in response.errors (background.js:131-176, particularly background.js:150 and background.js:157), but popup only shows a count: 'Started X of Y downloads. N items failed.' (popup.js:298-302).

User has no way to see WHICH media item failed or WHY. Also, popup.js:302 passes '' as the second arg to setStatus when there are failures, which gives neutral styling on partial success — should be a warning.

Fix:
- Surface at least the first error message: 'Media 2: <reason>'.
- Use a warning class on partial success.

Files: popup.js:279-303, background.js:159-176.

title: Tighten inflightDownloads dedupe to avoid stale settled-promise replay on rapid downloadAll retries
description:
The 5-second post-settle retention window in startDownloadWithInflight (background.js:420-432, especially the setTimeout at background.js:426) means that if a user clicks 'Download all media' twice within 5 seconds, the background may return the previously-settled promise instead of starting new downloads.

Repro scenarios:
- User triggers downloadAll, cancels Save As dialogs, clicks Download all again immediately → some items return the prior cancelled-rejection.
- User triggers downloadAll with partial success, retries → popup reports 'started downloads' without actually invoking chrome.downloads.download for the previously-succeeded items.

Fix options:
- A) Cache only while pending (delete on settle, no setTimeout).
- B) Delete rejected entries immediately, keep fulfilled briefly.
- C) Return an explicit deduped: true flag from startDownloadWithInflight when reusing a settled promise; popup adjusts wording.

Recommended: option B + C combined.

Files: background.js:420-432, background.js:147-156 (loop), popup.js:295-303 (status reporting).
